description.content:
An EventType is a registry entry representing a known event type. It
serves as the canonical reference that both EventExposures and
EventSubscriptions point to. EventTypes are identified by a dot-separated
identifier (e.g. "de.telekom.eni.quickstart.v1").

Find all registered EventTypes without any restrictions. 
This operation is global and not scoped to a specific application or team.

response.name: OK

description.content: Find an existing EventType by its complete identifier

description.content:
With an EventExposure you declare that your application publishes events
of a specific type. This makes the events available for subscription by
other applications. Each exposure is scoped to a zone and can define
visibility, approval strategy, and publisher-side scopes with filtering.
Find all EventExposures of this application

response.name: OK
OK

item.name: Find an existing EventExposure and return its status

request.name: Find an existing EventExposure and return its status

description.content: Find an existing EventExposure and return its status

item.name: Find all EventSubscriptions of this EventExposure

request.name: Find all EventSubscriptions of this EventExposure

description.content: Find all EventSubscriptions that subscribe to this EventExposure

response.name: OK

description.content:
An EventSubscription declares that your application wants to receive
events of a specific type. You can configure the delivery mechanism
(callback or server-sent events), payload format, and subscriber-side
filtering criteria.
Find all EventSubscriptions of this application

response.name: OK
OK

item.name: Find an existing EventSubscription and return its status

request.name: Find an existing EventSubscription and return its status

description.content: Find an existing EventSubscription and return its status

API of the TARDIS control plane for the eventing domain. With this API, you can manage your EventExposures and EventSubscriptions, and browse registered EventTypes.

This is a **read-only** API. You can only read the resources.
For creating and updating resources, you may use the Rover-API.

**Important:**
- You may use this API by subscribing to it by using the usual process.
You **do not** need to add `scopes` as the default scope is set to `tardis:user:read`.
If you require more permissions, please contact the TARDIS team.

- This is a Team-API and may be used by any team using TARDIS.
In the future, you can simply use your Team-credentials to access this API.


Contact Support:
 Name: TARDIS
 Email: FMB_TARDIS_Support@telekom.de
